items.title: Finke Desert Race legends reflect on race ahead of 50th anniversary

children.content: The 460-kilometre test of skill and endurance through the central Australian outback has become one of the most famous off-road races in the world.

items.title: Tour operators brace for Kakadu site closures 'like COVID all over again'

children.content: Parks Australia has announced popular Kakadu attractions Jim Jim Falls and Twin Falls will remain closed for the rest of the year, with one tourism operator saying the closures will lead to a "very hard" tourism season.

image.altText: An aerial shot of Jim Jim Falls

items.title: 'Gangsters' fuelled by alcohol. More problems in town camps revealed

children.content: After the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, the state of Alice Springs's town camps shocked the nation. Now, the man in charge of servicing them is speaking out.

gallery.altText: A love heart is drawn on a road into some gates.

thumbnail.altText: A love heart is drawn on a road into some gates.

items.title: Dramatic daylight arrest of NT fugitive, with police shooting car tyre

children.content: Northern Territory police have carried out a dramatic arrest of a 33-year-old man on a main Darwin road, saying the fugitive was wanted for a breach of parole.

image.altText: A police car blocking traffic on a main road in a tropical suburban area.

items.title: Hollywood legend set to bring Finke Desert Race to the big screen

children.content: The gruelling outback Finke Desert Race is set for Hollywood treatment after Marvel's Chris Hemsworth backs a feature-length movie.

image.altText: Chris Hemsworth stares into the camera.

items.title: Border Force busts NT multi-million-dollar illicit tobacco operation

children.content: Australian Border Force officers have seized hundreds of thousands of illegal cigarettes and vapes worth millions of dollars from a Northern Territory storage facility.

image.altText: A box with black packages with four cigarette packets lined up on top with one pack with cigarettes poking out of one packet

items.title: Almost one-third of NT children in care allegedly harmed, report finds

children.content: Almost one-third of children in out-of-home care in the Northern Territory were allegedly harmed in the last financial year, according to a new report from the Office of the Children's Commissioner.

image.altText: Children's toys, shoes, coloring pens.

items.title: NT scrutiny committee members call for shake-up after recommendations rejected

children.content: Non-government members of the Northern Territory Legislative Scrutiny Committee are calling for the body to be reformed, saying it is not operating the way it was intended.

gallery.altText: A parliamentary committee hears evidence from witnesses

thumbnail.altText: A parliamentary committee hears evidence from witnesses

items.title: Dieback disease killing mango trees is spreading in the NT

children.content: At a field day in the Northern Territory, mango growers were desperate to learn what could be done to stop the spread of mango twig tip dieback.

image.altText: Needles injected into mango trees

items.title: Teenage boy dies after allegedly stolen car rolls on major NT highway

children.content: A teenager has died after the allegedly stolen vehicle he was in rolled on a major Northern Territory highway early today.

image.altText: A vehicle parked across the highway, blocking traffic access, with a logo on the side that reads: ROAD OPERATIONS

items.title: Gas workers strike as negotiations on pay deal stall

children.content: Australian workers at the Japanese gas company Inpex walked off the job this morning, and plan to strike again this evening.

image.altText: Evening / dusk shot of Darwin harbour, industrial site, with yellow lights glowing and cranes in view.

items.title: Residents still can't eat too many fish from river in PFAS-affected town

children.content: Katherine residents have welcomed a $2 billion lawsuit over PFAS contamination in the town, saying "it's about time we started holding large corporations to account".

image.altText: Anjali Palmer stands outside a medical clinic in Katherine.

items.title: In a town where a baby died at a homeless campsite, charities say the data does not add up

children.content: As governments work to meet the growing need for social housing, community groups are stepping up to meet the rapidly increasing demand for their services to help fill the gap.

items.title: US–Australia talks on AUKUS changes have been underway for 18 months

children.content: The changes announced over the weekend will see Australia buy three second-hand submarines, rather than two second-hand and one new.

image.altText: Part of the hull and control room of a submarine that has surfaced from underwater.

items.title: Economic report says 82 occupations have been 'AI-disrupted'

children.content: A new economic report by consultancy firm Deloitte says the AI "winners" will be organisations that combine human and machine strengths, with recruiters saying human judgement will still be key.

articles.title: Australia joins exclusive club granting access to advanced AI

children.content: Fresh access to AI deemed too dangerous for public release has ramped up pressure on the government to respond to possible risks.

image.altText: Anthropic logo is seen in this photo

articles.title: What's got the pope and most Australians worried? This hyped new AI technology

children.content: Most Australians appear to distrust and dislike artificial intelligence despite using technology more than people in other countries.

children.content: An oil export terminal was hit hours before the Russian president's annual economic forum got underway.

items.title: 'Infuriating': Aboriginal voters turned away, told to line up again on SA election day

children.content: South Australia's Electoral Commission was aware that forcing First Nations voters to queue twice for the Voice election would be perceived as "discriminatory behaviour". Despite that, voters say it still happened and, in some cases, they were turned away.

image.altText: A woman wearing a maroon shirt and checked cardigan looks over her right shoulder.

items.title: Call to increase no-fish, no-drill zones as marine park review looms

children.content: Australia's dive industry has backed calls to expand no-fishing and no-drilling zones across the nation's oceans ahead of a federal review of marine parks.

items.title: Child sex offender given 'disability pride' award after admin error

children.content: Repeat child sex offender Cameron Bloomfield was crowned as Victoria's "disability pride champion" in 2024, after a government department admin error.

image.altText: Cameron Bloomfield stands outside in dappled light, smiling at the camera.

items.title: Ban on travelling under bridge being reviewed amid refuelling debacle

children.content: Authorities are considering whether it is feasible for Australia's Antarctic icebreaker to safely travel under Hobart's Tasman Bridge, as the Tasmanian government tries to resolve a costly refuelling saga.

items.title: Leanne Castley is already partyless but some Liberals want her gone entirely

children.content: Whether they're being driven away or choosing to jump in a getaway car, the women who have recently led the Canberra Liberals are no longer anywhere near the driver's seat.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer looks serious.

items.title: Cannabis driving limit change to give 'freedom' to medicinal users in NSW

children.content: The Labor government plans to introduce a legal limit on the concentration of THC a driver can have in their system if they have a prescription.

children.content: One of the world's biggest gambling companies is breaking into the 'rewards club' scene, raising the alarm among anti-gambling advocates.

items.title: NSW town may hold key for millions of Ukrainians needing mental support

children.content: Specialists from war-torn Ukraine are heading to regional New South Wales for trauma response and rehabilitation training.

image.altText: Woman in yellow and blue shirt, looks directly into camera with a neutral expression.

children.content: An application for a proposed puppy farm in central Victoria has been withdrawn after lobbying by nearby residents and animal activists.

items.title: Six charged over death and alleged kidnapping in Melbourne's west

children.content: Police raided several properties yesterday after the body of a man was found near a children's playground in Tarneit last month.

items.title: Heatwaves are becoming so extreme, they're 'breaking the biology' of plants

children.content: Record-breaking heatwaves decimated crops in Australia this year and served as a "real wake up call" for the urgent need to develop more heat-resistant crops and research into food security. But it's not only in summer heatwaves are occurring and causing crops to fail.

items.title: Rubio says concerns over Trump's health 'absurd and ridiculous'

children.content: The US president's mental acuity is in the spotlight, with the secretary of state shown videos of Donald Trump appearing to fall asleep during public appearances.

items.title: Indonesian official behind meals program plagued by food poisoning arrested

children.content: The ex-chief of Indonesia's free school meals program linked to food poisoning cases has been arrested after a corruption probe.
